skill.name: shareone
skill.description: Publish locally generated HTML pages, PDFs, or PPTX files to ShareOne platform and generate public sharing links (Capability URLs).
skill.summary: Upload files or conversation content to ShareOne and generate shareable links with optional password protection and watermarking.
skill.author: sudoprivacy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 163 patterns across 6 files, but most are false positives. External command detections in SKILL.md are documentation examples, not executable code. Network calls target only shareone.app (legitimate API integration). Filesystem and environment access patterns are expected for credential storage and file upload functionality. No malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Credential Storage in Home Directory
low_findings.description: API keys are stored in ~/.shareone_credentials file without encryption. This is standard practice but represents a low-risk exposure if the local system is compromised.

low_findings.title: Network API Calls to External Service
low_findings.description: Skill makes HTTP requests to shareone.app API endpoints. All network calls target the legitimate ShareOne service domain, which is expected functionality.

low_findings.title: Documentation Contains Command Examples
low_findings.description: SKILL.md includes bash command examples that triggered static analysis flags. These are documentation only, not executable code within the skill.

content.user_title: Publish files to ShareOne and generate shareable links
content.value_statement: Quickly share HTML pages, PDFs, or presentations by uploading to ShareOne platform with optional password protection. Get instant public links for your generated content without manual upload steps.
actual_capabilities: Upload HTML, PDF, PPTX, and text files to ShareOne platform
Generate public shareable links (Capability URLs) with configurable expiration
Add password protection and custom watermarks to shared content
Automatically create temporary API keys for new users
Update existing shares with new content or modified settings
Extract and share conversation content as formatted HTML pages
limitations: Requires ShareOne API key (automatic guest key creation limited to 5 per day)
Binary files use S3 direct upload which may have size limitations
Shared links expire after 90 days unless extended
Cannot upload files that fail ShareOne content policy screening

use_cases.title: Share Generated Reports
use_cases.description: Upload analysis reports or generated documentation and share with team members via secure links.

use_cases.title: Distribute Presentations
use_cases.description: Convert local presentation files to shareable links with password protection for client delivery.

use_cases.title: Share Code Snippets and Conversations
use_cases.description: Extract conversation content or code blocks as formatted HTML pages for easy sharing.

prompt_templates.title: Basic File Upload
prompt_templates.prompt: Publish the file index.html to ShareOne and give me a shareable link.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick upload of a single file without additional options

prompt_templates.title: Password Protected Share
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload report.pdf to ShareOne with password 'confidential2026' and generate a link.
prompt_templates.scenario: Sharing sensitive documents that require access control

prompt_templates.title: Share Conversation Content
prompt_templates.prompt: Take the code you just generated and publish it as a shareable HTML page on ShareOne.
prompt_templates.scenario: Converting conversation output into a persistent shareable format

prompt_templates.title: Update Existing Share
prompt_templates.description: Advanced workflow for updating previously shared content
prompt_templates.prompt: Update the existing ShareOne link for index.html with the new content and add watermark 'Draft v2'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Modifying an already-published share with new content or settings

output_examples.input: Publish index.html to ShareOne with password 'secret123'
output_examples.output: Publish successful! Your file is now available at: https://shareone.app/s/abc123xyz. Access password: secret123

output_examples.input: Share the report PDF with my team
output_examples.output: Report uploaded successfully. Share link: https://shareone.app/pdf/def456uvw. Tip: You can also add a custom watermark to protect your content.

best_practices: Always use password protection when sharing sensitive or internal documents
Add descriptive watermarks to identify document ownership and prevent unauthorized redistribution
Save your API key securely to avoid repeated authentication prompts during a session
anti_patterns: Do not share links publicly without password protection for confidential content
Avoid uploading binary files through the pages API - use the files endpoint instead
Do not ignore the 90-day retention policy when planning long-term document distribution

faq.question: What is a ShareOne Capability URL?
faq.answer: A Capability URL is a persistent, shareable link that grants access to your uploaded content for a defined period (typically 90 days). It encodes access permissions directly in the URL path.

faq.question: Do I need to create a ShareOne account to use this skill?
faq.answer: No. The skill can automatically create a temporary guest API key for you (limited to 5 creations per day). For long-term use, register at shareone.app to get a permanent API key.

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: HTML, Markdown, text files, PDF, PPTX, and common image formats (PNG, JPG, GIF). HTML and text use the pages API, while binary files use S3 direct upload.

faq.question: How long do shared links remain active?
faq.answer: Shared content is retained for 90 days by default. You can manage and extend retention through your ShareOne account dashboard.

faq.question: Can I update content after publishing?
faq.answer: Yes. If you share the same file again in a session, the skill detects the existing share_id and performs an update (PUT) instead of creating a new share.

faq.question: What happens if my content fails the safety check?
faq.answer: ShareOne performs automated content screening. If blocked, you will receive an error message with the specific reason. Content violating policies cannot be published.
